professional_summary.overview: AI Engineer with hands-on experience integrating AI solutions into scalable applications and optimizing machine learning workflows. Proficient in Python, CI/CD pipelines, and cloud deployment on Azure, with a strong foundation in deep learning and computer vision. Adept at collaborating across functions to develop innovative, end-to-end AI systems and automate complex workflows.
key_highlights: Integrating AI solutions into scalable applications and optimizing machine learning workflows.
Proficient in Python, CI/CD pipelines, and cloud deployment on Azure and AWS.
Developing innovative end-to-end AI systems and automating complex workflows.
Strong foundation in deep learning and computer vision for real-world applications.
Adept at collaborating across functions to deliver high-impact AI solutions.
Designing secure Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architectures for sensitive data.
Orchestrating distributed AI workloads using Ray for high-throughput inference.
Implementing full-stack observability for monitoring LLM and system performance.

responsibilities: Engineered secure Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architectures using LangChain and AWS Bedrock, enabling real-time retrieval of operational data restricted by fine-grained user access levels.

Built low-latency vector retrieval layers in Python using FAISS and Pinecone, incorporating security-aware meta-data filtering to ensure data privacy and compliance across internal troubleshooting guides.
Developed Agent Assist intelligence that surfaces context-aware recommendations to service providers based on specific authorization profiles, improving support accuracy and organizational security.
Optimized and deployed open-source LLMs like Llama 2 and Mistral within internal AI workflows, implementing data isolation protocols for automated call summarization and sensitive query resolution.

Provisioned and managed cloud-native infrastructure using Terraform and AWS EKS, enforcing strict authentication with AWS IAM and Secrets Manager to safeguard sensitive enterprise data and operational secrets.

Orchestrated distributed AI workloads using Python and Ray on AWS, delivering high-throughput, low-latency inference for large-scale summarization and knowledge synthesis across multi-tenant datasets.
Established full-stack observability with Prometheus, Grafana, and Evidently AI to monitor system latency, retrieval performance, and LLM response quality within a secured production environment.
Automated the end-to-end ML lifecycle using MLflow, DVC, and Apache Airflow, coordinating secure data ingestion and versioning to ensure reproducibility and audit compliance for internal AI services.

professional_experience.role: Graduate Research Assistant and Teaching Assistant
responsibilities: Engineered and optimized high-throughput data pipelines for Wide-Area Motion Imagery (WAMI) and event camera research; automated the ingestion, cleaning, and preprocessing of high-volume virtual environment data (Unreal Engine/CARLA) using NumPy and Pandas.

Designed and implemented a proprietary multi-drone image stitching solution for thesis work, utilizing Light Field Dynamic Homography (LDH). Employed advanced feature matching techniques (SIFT/SURF) and integrated the computer vision pipeline with Keras and Transformers for high-resolution panoramic generation.

Developed scalable data evaluation frameworks and optimized model deployment and inference across research projects using C++, Python, R, and Matlab, demonstrating proficiency in low-level computational efficiency critical for real-time WAMI and drone swarm coordination.
Collaborated with interdisciplinary research teams to document experimental results and present technical findings in academic forums.
Mentored undergraduate students in computer science fundamentals, including data structures, algorithms, and Python programming.
Configured and maintained high-performance computing (HPC) environments for distributed machine learning training sessions.
Developed custom scripts to automate the labeling and validation of synthetic datasets generated from CARLA simulations.
Assisted in the grading and instruction of graduate-level courses focused on artificial intelligence and machine learning principles.

Developed and deployed a high-performance product recommendation engine for e-commerce, implementing Singular Value Decomposition and Factorization Machines to enhance user personalization and engagement metrics.

Architected end-to-end ETL pipelines using Databricks and Apache Spark to process high-throughput data streams, ensuring seamless data ingestion from diverse sources into a centralized lakehouse for downstream modeling.

Performed rigorous Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) and built interactive data visualization dashboards to identify consumer behavior patterns, utilizing statistical insights to refine feature engineering and model accuracy.

Established robust MLOps workflows by integrating MLflow for experiment tracking and Jenkins for CI/CD, automating the model lifecycle from initial training to production deployment on cloud infrastructure.

Optimized backend microservices using Java and Spring Boot to serve real-time model inferences, utilizing Docker containerization and Terraform for scalable resource provisioning across AWS and Azure environments.
Collaborated with blockchain developers to integrate smart contract event data into machine learning models for fraud detection.
Improved system reliability by implementing comprehensive unit and integration tests for recommendation engine microservices.
Participated in sprint planning and code reviews to maintain high standards of code quality and architectural integrity.

Developed and delivered two data-centric web applications (Parking Information System and Event Management Platform) using a full-stack approach including HTML, CSS, JavaScript, AngularJS, PHP, and Java Servlets.

Established and managed underlying SQL databases, implementing efficient data retrieval methods and setting up basic web-based data pipelines, showcasing foundational experience in real-time data handling.
Fully developed the Event Management Web Application remotely, demonstrating strong independent development skills and the ability to deliver complex, responsive solutions from front-end to back-end.
Designed and refined user interface components to improve accessibility and user engagement across web platforms.
Collaborated with senior developers to troubleshoot and resolve backend logical errors and database bottlenecks.
Drafted comprehensive technical documentation and user manuals for the newly developed Parking Information System.
Implemented secure user authentication and authorization modules using session management and encrypted credentials.
Conducted thorough testing of application features across multiple browsers to ensure cross-platform compatibility.

projects.name: MULTI-DRONE VIRTUAL CAMERA GENERATION (Thesis Project)
description: Designed and implemented a multi-drone aerial imaging system in AirSim, utilizing its physics simulation for optimized drone swarm flight paths, capturing high-resolution, time-synced images across large areas.

Developed an optimized image stitching pipeline using Light-Field Dynamic Homography (LDH), achieving superior alignment and minimal overlap compared to standard methods by leveraging advanced feature matching (SIFT/SURF) for precise homography estimation.

Successfully generated and validated high-resolution stitched panoramic views, processing sequential image data using advanced structures like Transformers and demonstrating expertise in computer vision for remote sensing.

Integrated an iterative optimization algorithm (LDH) into the stitching pipeline to refine camera poses and homography matrices post-initial estimation, minimizing accumulated registration errors across multiple drone frames.

Developed and utilized a custom software abstraction layer in C++ and Python to interface with AirSim’s low-level controls, implementing Visual Servoing to control and synchronize four virtual drones for accurate data capture.

Constructed a novel synthetic dataset of large-area aerial scenes from the simulated environment, complete with ground-truth depth maps, used for quantitative evaluation of LDH against traditional stitching methods under varying conditions.

projects.name: CREATING FARMER COLLABORATION COMMUNITIES USING SOCIAL NETWORK
description: Developed and deployed the entire social network platform using the MEAN stack (AngularJS, NodeJS, MongoDB) to facilitate the real-time sharing of crop disease updates and preventive measures.

Implemented a scalable CI/CD pipeline via CircleCI to automate integration and delivery of application updates. The full stack was containerized using Docker, ensuring a consistent and rapid deployment process.

Engineered robust backend services in NodeJS to manage user authentication, data synchronization, and dynamic feed/chat features based on specific crop types, demonstrating strong proficiency in backend development and asynchronous operations.
Successfully launched a functional, collaborative platform that provided farmers with a user-friendly mechanism for real-time agricultural information exchange.

projects.name: CROP DISEASE DETECTION USING CONVENTIONAL NEURAL NETWORK
description: Developed and trained a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for early-stage crop disease prediction, utilizing frameworks like Keras/TensorFlow and implementing transfer learning to accelerate convergence and achieve high performance.

Managed the end-to-end image data pipeline, involving collecting, performing augmentation, normalization, and rigorous preprocessing using NumPy and OpenCV to enhance data quality and model generalization.
Achieved a quantifiable high accuracy (e.g., 96% on a held-out set) in disease identification, validating deep expertise in applied deep learning for agricultural AI.

Integrated this model into the Farmer collaboration community project, hosting the application and model on Amazon Web Services (AWS) Free Tier. Utilized advanced web sockets for secure, dynamic application connectivity, creating a unified and impactful solution.

projects.name: CHURN PREDICTION
description: Designed and implemented a customer churn prediction model for the banking sector, evaluating multiple machine learning algorithms where Logistic Regression delivered the optimal balance of performance and interpretability.
Engineered features from transaction and customer metadata to enable proactive identification of customers likely to switch accounts, directly supporting customer retention strategies.
Developed a robust data preparation and feature selection workflow, reinforcing strong skills in applied Machine Learning and data preprocessing techniques.

projects.name: BOOK YOUR TICKETS
description: Developed a decentralized ticket booking platform using Solidity for smart contract creation and MetaMask for secure wallet integration.
Enabled transparent, tamper-proof ticket purchases and ownership verification through blockchain technology.
Gained strong skills in full-stack web development and secure, decentralized application design, reinforcing expertise in distributed systems architecture.
